name: Bittersweet Chocolate Coated Truffles
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Peanut Oil or Almond Oil
recipeInstructions:
Directions: For Truffles:
1. In a double boiler, melt the broken chocolate, stirring constantly. Alternatively, microwave on medium power for 2 to 3 minutes.
2. Remove chocolate from heat; blend in butter.
3. Stir in cream and liqueur until well combined.
4. Combine with chopped nuts or candied fruit if desired (optional).
5. Chill for 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently until thick enough to hold a shape.
6. Drop by heaping teaspoon or #70 scoop onto foil-lined baking pan.
7. Shape the truffles into rounds if desired.
8. Cover and freeze for 20-30 minutes to set them firm before dipping.
For Dipping:
1. Melt chocolate as directed in the basic truffle recipe.
2. Remove from heat and stir in oil.
3. Cool chocolate to a temperature of 85-90°F (29-32°C) for dipping.
4. Dip cold, firm truffles into melted chocolate, holding with a fork or spooning several coats of chocolate.
5. Place each dipped truffle onto foil-lined baking pan.
6. Decorate the top with nuts, candied fruit, etc., if desired.
7. Chill at least 2 hours to set the chocolate.
8. Optionally, roll in additional Ghirardelli Ground Chocolate.
9. Store in a cool, dry place for several days before serving.
